posts.body_html:
CyberClaims investigators have recently identified a growing network of high-risk websites that show clear warning signs of fraudulent activity. While many of these platforms appear professional and trustworthy at first glance, they are very likely designed to deceive users, collect personal information and ultimately steal funds. We strongly advise anyone browsing or engaging with unfamiliar online platforms to proceed with extreme caution, especially those requesting deposits, crypto transfers or sensitive documents.

If you have already interacted with any suspicious website, stop communication immediately and avoid sharing any additional details. Consider reporting the domain to your local cybercrime authority, and seek guidance from reputable dispute-resolution professionals who can assess your situation and help you take the next steps.

Online scams continue to evolve, making due diligence essential. Always verify the legitimacy of a platform before creating an account, entering personal data or sending any money. Protecting yourself starts with awareness.

posts.title: Is Reclaimoria.com A Scam?
posts.body_html:
The feeling of being cheated out of your money online can be incredibly disheartening. After losing money on an investment platform (which may be a scam perpetrated through a deceptive romantic relationship) or by means of a shady trading approach, many folks initially seek assistance from others to find alternatives to recover their money. Typically these individuals will turn to the internet looking for recovery sites to assist them in recovering their funds. For many, the first places they go searching for assistance will be websites such as Reclaimoria.com. Initially, these sites may look appealing and provide information that sounds encouraging. However, once you dig deeper into these sites, cracks in their credibility become apparent.

The recovery scam is one of the lowest forms of scams available. In this case, they take advantage of those people who have already been taken advantage of and are therefore even more cruel. Sadly, Reclaimoria exemplifies what I just described almost perfectly. (How to Spot an Online Scam)

How Reclaimoria Describes Their Work

Reclaimoria is a company that claims to provide a way for people who have lost money in online scams to recover their lost funds. Their site is well-designed with a friendly look, making it easy for someone in a stressful situation to feel comfortable about using their service. They advertise their expertise in Crypto Fund Recovery and how they are experts at tracing and recovering funds from fraudulent websites. They suggest they use special methods to recover funds quickly.

This advertising message can be very appealing when you are lost, angry, and looking for anything to help you recover your money.

They suggest for you to give them your personal information to allow a “specialist” to begin working on your case right away. They make it sound like you already have assistance on the way.

This is the same type of information we continue to see lack of trust in recovery scamming sites.

How Reclaimoria Is A Scam To Obtain Someone's Valuable Property Back

Almost every sign of a fraud asset recovery scam is shown by Reclaimoria. These types of scammers often prey on victims who have already been ripped-off. In fact many of the same criminals that ripped-off a person initially often return under a different name and claim to 'help' the victim get their money back.

We see frequently scammers create the impression of a successful return of their money by posing as a victim that had their money returned and try to get real victims to believe they are real by having their scam company post on scam forums.

The scamming companies often use various names. Some are referred to as 'refund agents' some as 'fund recovery specialists' and some 'digital investigation specialists'. However, the way they work is always the same; they capitalize on the fear, anxiety and desperation of their victims.

With the growth of the crypto scam market, these types of scams are increasing at an alarming rate. (Reclaimoria-style scams)

Although it Appears to Be Well Made, This Site Could Be a Scam

Even though websites like Reclaimoria are made to look as if they belong to well respected businesses, people often believe that they are legitimate because of their appearances. The site seems to be professionally designed, has friendly language, and appears simple and well organized. Therefore, after being scammed, the design and appearance of these types of sites can lead you to a false sense of security.

Do not rush into making an investment or decision about any potential losses because a professional investigation will require time and experience. No one can guarantee that they will recover money from a cryptocurrency investment in a short period of time. No one can magically undo any transactions made on the blockchain. Anyone who claims that they will recover your lost cryptocurrency within hours or days is dishonest.

Reclaimoria uses phrases like "immediate recovery" and "fast tracking." These phrases should cause alarm for consumers in this industry.

Tech Jargon is Nonsense

The Reclaimoria website uses lots of technical jargon to make themselves appear "high-tech." They mention using advanced tools, performing deep blockchain analysis and detailed tracing systems.

If the descriptions were something that could be believed, fine. But they are not.

Many of the technical terms used by Reclaimoria are ambiguous, or referred to equipment that can never be available for consumer purchase in this way. We have seen scores of other fake recovery companies use similar descriptions of their operations. They are simply attempting to impress emotionally distressed individuals.

Legitimate investigators do not need to disguise behind jargon. They provide plain English descriptions of their procedures. If something seems overly verbose or lacks clarity, then it tends to suggest that the company is a scam. (Online Fraud Information)

They Ask For Information That Can Put You At Even Greater Risk

One of the biggest concerns with Reclaimoria is the type of information they encourage you to submit. The forms ask for personal details, transaction logs, wallet screenshots, phone numbers, and email addresses.

To someone who just wants help, this might feel normal. But this is where victims get exposed to even more danger.

We’ve seen many cases where information shared with a fake recovery service was later used for:

• additional scams
• phishing attacks
• identity theft
• social engineering attempts
• new scam approaches under different company names

A real investigative team treats data with respect and explains exactly why certain details are required. Scammers simply gather everything they can and use it however they want.

What To Do If You Already Shared Information With Reclaimoria

If you’ve already submitted your details, don’t panic. You still have steps you can take to protect yourself.

Do the following as soon as possible:

• Change the passwords of any accounts connected to the information you shared
• Enable two factor authentication everywhere you can
• Secure your email and review your recent login history
• Contact your bank if you shared payment details
• Watch your accounts for unexpected activity
• Keep copies of everything you sent them
• Do not reply to any more messages from them
• Get advice from professionals who know how recovery scams operate

The faster you act, the better your chances of preventing further harm.

How CyberClaims Helps Victims Of Recovery Scams

The first thing we do when someone approaches us for help with their experience through Reclaimoria or any similar website is to review the communication and any data or information that was shared with the scammers, including any reports that were given to them by the scammers. Recovery scams generally have a clear pattern of behaviour to follow, and it is easy for us to determine what is genuinely happening when we assess the evidence from our assessment of the information that was provided to us.

The second part of our process is the analysis of the digital evidence. We analyse the digital evidence collected from victims, including their wallet activity, transaction histories, email communications, etc. This helps us understand what the scammer's true intentions were all along. With this information, we provide victims with guidance on how to determine whether their private information has been compromised and what precautionary steps they can take going forward.

We base everything we do on credible evidence and realism; we do not make false claims nor promise you a quick fix or a miracle. All we offer are realistic solutions with the support and guidance of qualified professionals.

Bring What You Have Lost Back to You

You might have thought that Reclaimoria was a legitimate service when you first came across them; however, after doing some digging and research, it is clear that there are many red flags associated with this website. The website's overly large promises, confusing technical jargon, and request for sensitive personal information mean that it is likely a scam designed to take advantage of people trying to recover their losses.

The reason recovery scams like this are so damaging is because they exploit fear and hope at the same time, thereby taking advantage of consumers. The more consumers learn about recovery scams and how they operate, the less likely those customers will be able to fall victim to these scams.

If you have been contacted by Reclaimoria or any other recovery service and you are not sure about what you should do next, please contact CyberClaims. We will assess your case, explain what is actually happening, and help you navigate the whole process. You do not have to go through this alone, and you don’t have to be confused about what actions to take next.

posts.title: Exposed: The Truth About Ne-xperts.com and How to Recover Your Funds
posts.body_html:
Experts in cryptocurrency fraud and recovering lost funds, we at Cyberclaims.net witness serious harm from fake investments every day. Right now, attention turns to Ne-xperts (ne-xperts.com) - a site often marked risky by alert users and professionals alike. Spotted this firm? Then treat this as an immediate caution.

Your goal is this: we give facts, while guiding toward healing when harm’s occurred.

🚨 Multiple Red Flags: Why Ne-xperts.com is Untrustworthy

Our review, along with several separate findings, points to repeated warning signs typical of fake investing sites.

  1. Complete Lack of Transparency and Regulation
    Reputable finance companies clearly show who owns them and their licenses. In contrast, Ne-xperts does not
  • Hidden owners: This website hides registration details while offering zero traceable company data.
    No financial license – runs without approval from trusted regulators such as the FCA, SEC, or ASIC. As a result, your money isn't covered by law.
  1. Official Warnings and Blacklisting
    The platform’s actions caught attention - observers took note without delay; its presence sparked reactions across different circles due to visible impact

· Public scam alerts have been issued, explicitly warning investors: "Do not invest any money with a company called Ne-xperts".
It was added to a blacklist by financial watchdogs due to fraud, while also tied to issues around fund withdrawals.

  1. Pattern of Complaints
    Customers repeatedly mention problems typical of fraudulent schemes

· Withdrawal Issues: Some accounts get frozen; others face rejected requests due to high charges or constant delays labeled as "under review." .

  • Intense pressure on users: it advertises big crypto gains to attract deposits, yet fails to explain the risks involved. Despite promises of quick profits, clear warnings are missing from its messaging.

🔍 How These Scams Operate and Why They Succeed

Ne-xperts matches what’s known as an offshore unregulated broker. Such firms work beyond legal oversight, so recovering funds becomes extremely difficult. Instead of real credibility, they rely on polished sites along with fabricated reviews to appear trustworthy.

A troubling pattern has appeared - scam survivors face new frauds through fake recovery offers. When people lose money on sites such as Ne-xperts, others may exploit their situation by pretending to help get funds back. These repeat cons show how essential it is to pick a refund provider with clear proof of legitimacy and open processes.

✅ What to Do If You've Been Scammed by Ne-xperts

If withdrawing funds isn't possible, act fast - every moment counts. Take action now without delay

  1. Document Everything

Keep every message - save emails along with chat records.
Store transaction IDs alongside wallet details along with transfer data from banks.
· Take screenshots of your account dashboard and withdrawal denials.

  1. Report the Scam

Submit a report to your regional finance authority or police department. Use this step if you face monetary issues locally.
Notify fraud-monitoring sites about the broker - this helps protect future users.

  1. Explore Recovery Avenues

Contact your bank - or get in touch with your card issuer - to ask about possibly reversing a transaction.
Get help from an authorized company that specializes in recovering funds.

🤝 How Cyberclaims Provides a Legitimate Path to Recovery

At Cyberclaims, our team delivers clear support to people affected by cryptocurrency or investment scams. Because shady firms hide details, we focus on openness - just like feedback from those we’ve helped shows. Our approach differs; it’s built on trust rather than promises.

Our Proven Process:

  • Complimentary case evaluation: First, we conduct a private review - no commitment needed - to understand your circumstances.
  • Crypto tracking and analysis: With top-tier blockchain tools, we follow stolen assets to uncover where they exit and who’s responsible.
  • Clear reporting: Our thorough analysis helps back claims in court or challenge decisions by banks.
  • Personalized help: Work alongside a committed advisor who supports you at each stage.

See for yourself - clients describe working with us like this:

"Cyberclaims handled my case with great care and attention… They clearly said it might not work out, but it actually did."
"Full recovery!! Darren Kaplan and the recovery team at Cyberclaims were amazing. We lost over 300K to a crypto trading scam… I’m feeling really grateful."
"They were professional, honest, and managed to recover a portion of my funds. I would highly recommend them."

🛡️ Protect Yourself and Take Action

The tale behind Ne-xperts.com serves as a clear warning - check any firm’s official standing prior to putting money in. Whenever a deal seems unrealistically profitable, chances are high it isn't legitimate.

If you’ve had money stolen by Ne-xperts.com or a site like it, there’s still a chance to recover it. Help exists - yet speed matters, along with picking the right team carefully.

Begin healing now alongside a reliable team. Reach out to Cyberclaims - they offer private, no-cost advice right away.

posts.title: Is Recovery-Means.com a Scam?
posts.body_html:
What You Should Know Before Trusting Them

When you’ve been the victim of an online scam, your biggest fear is getting trapped again. The emotions that accompany the loss of funds lead thousands of people to seek immediate assistance after suffering the loss. The first thing they typically search for on the internet is “crypto recovery services” or “fund recovery.”

That’s where sites such as Recovery-Means.com come into play.

The Recovery-Means.com website has an extremely professional and clean appearance. The site mentions digital tracing, fund recovery, and that they utilize “advanced investigative tools.” If you are in pain due to the loss of your investment or feeling overwhelmed or desperate following your loss, then the language used on this site provides you with some comfort. You will feel as though there is someone out there who understands what you are going through.

Once you stop and really evaluate the Recovery-Means.com site, you will begin to realize that the entire scenario has changed.

Based on a recent independent domain security examination of Recovery-Means.com, this site has been designated a critical-risk website with a danger score of ninety percent. This warning is very serious and not to be taken lightly.

How Recovery-Means.com Presents Itself

Recovery-Means.com claims that they specialize in helping those affected by online scams recover their funds. Their site provides information about tracing digital footprints (fingerprints), conducting cyber forensic examinations, and performing thorough investigations.

The home page of Recovery-Means, as shown on both a mobile phone and desktop screen, appears very professional and credible; they state that they provide support and "expert advice" to assist those impacted by a scam.

To individuals who are feeling overwhelmed, this type of marketing and approach appears as though it is a ray of hope.

On the Recovery-Means website, they urge visitors to send their "personal information" to initiate immediate action by a "specialist" to recover funds. The way the company presents itself creates an illusion of quickness, ease, and assurance regarding recovery.

It’s not uncommon for recovery scam websites to make such extravagant promises.

Why Recovery-Means.com Raises Serious Red Flags

The domain security report doesn’t just raise concerns, it outright labels the website as a critical threat and advises users not to visit it at all:

“DO NOT VISIT this website under any circumstances.”

Here’s what the report uncovered:

1. Extremely New Domain (Very High Risk)

The domain is only 129 days old, making it fall squarely into the high-risk category for fraudulent sites .

Scammers typically use newly registered domains so they can shut them down quickly and switch to new ones when they get flagged.

2. Missing Legal and Regulatory Information

The site has:

• no terms of service
• unverified claims of regulation
• suspicious-looking testimonials

Legitimate recovery firms cannot operate without transparency. Recovery-Means.com offers none.

3. No History on the Wayback Machine

This means the site has no established record or web history, another common sign of a temporary scam domain.

4. Similar Suspicious Domains Detected

The security report found related variants like recoverymeans.org and recoverymeans.com, which is typical of scam clusters that copy each other.

All these signs match the playbook of recovery scammers.

The Tech Jargon Doesn’t Mean Anything

Hostile recovery sites will generally use a lot of terminology that sounds impressive on the outside, i.e. "digital footprints" & "complete investigation procedure". The security research indicates that a large portion of the claims made by these sites are unverifiable & not consistent with what would actually be done in a typical investigation setting.

This happens all the time.

Recovery sites are typically using elaborate terminology in order to create confusion among their victims and create a more polished appearance. A true investigator does not do that, they clearly explain what they are doing without utilizing buzzwords.

If a site or company cannot explain their methodology in plain language, there is a good chance the methods do not actually exist.

They Ask for Information That Could Put You in More Danger

Just like other recovery scams, Recovery-Means.com asks for:

• personal details
• phone number
• email address
• wallet screenshots
• transaction logs

At a moment when you’re already desperate for help, this might feel normal.

But the report confirms that this website should not be trusted with any sensitive information. Even the act of visiting the site was flagged as unsafe, let alone handing over personal data.

What To Do If You Already Contacted Recovery-Means.com

If you already submitted information, don’t panic, but act quickly. Here’s what you should do:

• Change passwords linked to the email or accounts you shared
• Enable two-factor authentication everywhere
• Notify your bank if you shared financial details
• Keep a record of everything you sent
• Stop replying to their emails or calls
• Watch for unusual login attempts
• Get genuine assistance from professionals

The faster you secure your information, the safer you’ll be.

How CyberClaims Can Help You

When someone contacts CyberClaims about a suspicious recovery site like Recovery-Means.com, our team reviews all communication, screenshots, and evidence provided by the victim.

Because these scams follow very specific patterns, we can quickly determine what has happened and what risks are involved.

Next, we analyse digital evidence such as wallet activity, transaction movements, and messages exchanged with the scammers. This helps us understand what the scammers were really trying to do and how far the exposure may have gone.

Our approach is simple:
no false promises, no shortcuts, no guaranteed overnight recoveries.

We focus on clarity, transparency, and professional guidance based on evidence, not empty claims.

Final Thoughts

At first glance, Recovery-Means.com might look like a legitimate recovery service. Its website is professionally designed, the language sounds helpful, and the visuals seem polished. But once you peel back the layers and examine the facts, the red flags are impossible to ignore.

A newly registered domain, missing legal documentation, unverifiable claims, fake-looking testimonials, and a critical-risk security rating all point to the same conclusion:
Recovery-Means.com should not be trusted.

Recovery scams like this take advantage of fear and hope at the same time. But the more you understand how they operate, the less power they have over you.

If you believe you have been contacted by Recovery-Means.com or a similar operation, reach out to CyberClaims. We’ll review your case, explain what’s really going on, and guide you through your next steps, clearly and safely.

You’re not alone in this, and you don’t need to navigate the situation blindly.

posts.title: Is CyberClaims Legit?
posts.body_html:
Is CyberClaims Legit?

Many people search for help after losing money to a crypto scam, but the internet is full of fake “recovery agents” and misleading promises. It is natural to question whether a service like CyberClaims is legitimate.
The short answer is yes. CyberClaims is a real, registered, and transparent dispute-resolution service that helps victims trace stolen funds and submit formal recovery claims with regulated institutions.

Below is a clear explanation of what makes CyberClaims legitimate, how it operates, and how clients can verify it independently.

A Registered and Transparent Company

CyberClaims is operated by Transparent Business Solutions B.V., a legally registered European entity. This means the company has a traceable structure, verified registration details, and clear accountability, unlike anonymous recovery scams often found on social media.

Key indicators of legitimacy include:

  • A verified business entity with publicly available registration
  • A real website at CyberClaims.net with full service information
  • Publicly published case studies and results
  • A clear service agreement outlining the process and expectations

There is no hidden team, no anonymous communication, and no unrealistic claims.

Clear and Ethical Service Approach

Legitimate recovery services do not guarantee results, request crypto payments, or insist on rushed decisions. CyberClaims follows a professional, step-by-step workflow based on evidence and compliance procedures.

CyberClaims does not:

  • Claim special access to police, banks, or governments
  • Promise guaranteed recovery
  • Ask clients to send crypto to “unlock” or “verify” funds
  • Operate through anonymous WhatsApp or Telegram accounts

Instead, the team uses genuine investigative tools and formal communication channels with exchanges and payment providers.

Evidence-Based Dispute Resolution

CyberClaims focuses on data-driven recovery, not speculation.
The process typically involves:

  1. Initial assessment to confirm whether the case is actionable
  2. Blockchain tracing to follow the movement of stolen crypto
  3. Detailed evidence reports that financial institutions take seriously
  4. Formal dispute submissions to exchanges or payment processors
  5. Structured follow-ups with compliance departments

This is a recognized method within the financial services sector. It does not rely on false guarantees, it relies on documented evidence and regulatory obligations.

Documented Results and Public Case Examples

CyberClaims publishes real case studies on its website, including a verified recovery of over €90,000 for a victim of a crypto job scam. These cases are written transparently to help visitors understand:

  • What happened
  • How the dispute was handled
  • What steps led to recovery
  • What clients can realistically expect

Fake recovery services do not publish verifiable results or attach their name to real outcomes. CyberClaims does.

Why Asking This Question Matters

The crypto recovery space is full of imposters. Many victims are approached by fake agents who pretend to help but end up stealing even more money.
Asking “Is CyberClaims legit?” is not only reasonable it is essential.

CyberClaims encourages clients to verify the company, ask questions, and read through the service structure before beginning any paid work.

Summary

CyberClaims is a legitimate, EU-registered dispute-resolution service that assists victims of crypto and online investment scams using professional tracing and evidence-based procedures.
It operates transparently, offers realistic expectations, and provides documented results.

posts.title: How Does CyberClaims Work?
posts.body_html:
When someone becomes a victim of a crypto scam, the situation can feel complex and overwhelming. CyberClaims was created to bring structure and clarity to that moment. Rather than offering unrealistic guarantees, the service focuses on evidence, compliance and formal procedures that can genuinely influence the outcome of a recovery case.

Here is a clear look at how CyberClaims operates and what clients can expect throughout the process.

Initial Case Assessment

Every case begins with a review of the information the victim provides. This typically includes screenshots, transaction IDs, wallet addresses and a description of how the scam unfolded.
The purpose of this stage is simple: to determine whether the case has real recovery potential. CyberClaims is selective, and not every case is accepted. If the evidence shows that recovery is unlikely, the team will communicate that honestly before any paid work begins.

This early assessment ensures transparency and prevents victims from investing further into situations where no meaningful action is possible.

Tracing What Happened on the Blockchain

Once a case is accepted, CyberClaims begins the tracing process. Although crypto can feel anonymous, every transaction leaves a permanent digital footprint. By analyzing these public records with forensic tools, CyberClaims follows the movement of the stolen funds through the blockchain.

This investigation reveals where the money was sent, which wallets were involved and whether the assets eventually reached a regulated exchange or service provider.
In many cases, understanding this flow is the key to identifying where a dispute can be filed and where compliance teams may still be able to act.

Preparing a Structured Evidence Report

After the tracing phase, CyberClaims prepares a formal report that explains the case clearly and professionally. It outlines the scam, the movement of funds, the wallet connections and any regulatory concerns that may apply.

This report is written in a format that financial institutions, banks and exchanges recognize and take seriously. Instead of a general complaint or an emotional message, the institution receives a documented fraud case supported by technical evidence and a timeline of events.

Submitting Disputes to Exchanges and Payment Services

With the report complete, CyberClaims contacts the relevant institutions. Depending on the case, this may involve international crypto exchanges, fiat on-ramp providers or banking partners connected to the transaction flow.

The goal at this stage is to request actions such as reviewing the receiving account, freezing funds that are still accessible, or investigating suspicious activity.
Institutions are far more responsive when they receive structured evidence, and CyberClaims’ approach is designed to meet the standards required by compliance departments.

Ongoing Follow-Up and Communication

Recovery rarely happens from a single message. It is a process that requires consistent follow-up.
CyberClaims manages all communication with institutions, responds to their questions, provides additional documentation when needed and keeps the client informed of all updates.

This ongoing dialogue is one of the most important parts of the process. Persistence, clarity and professionalism often lead to results that would not be achievable through a one-time complaint.

Final Outcome and Next Steps

When the dispute cycle is complete, CyberClaims provides a summary of what was done and what was discovered.
This may include information about any frozen accounts, compliance responses or recovery steps that were successful. Even in cases where full recovery is not possible, clients receive detailed documentation that can be useful for regulators or law enforcement.

Transparency is a core part of this stage. Clients are never left guessing about what happened behind the scenes.

What CyberClaims Does Not Do

It is equally important to understand what CyberClaims does not offer.
The service does not claim to hack wallets, recover funds instantly or work with law enforcement on the client’s behalf. It does not request crypto payments or make promises of guaranteed results.

The entire process is based on legitimate, established methods: tracing, documentation and compliance-driven dispute resolution.

Summary

CyberClaims works through a structured, professional approach that includes case evaluation, blockchain tracing, formal reporting, dispute filing and consistent follow-up with financial institutions.
The goal is to give victims a realistic and evidence-based path toward recovering stolen funds.

For anyone unsure of what to do after a crypto scam, CyberClaims.net provides a clear and credible starting point.

posts.title: What Services Does CyberClaims Offer?
posts.body_html:
After falling victim to a crypto scam, most people are left with more questions than answers. Where did the money go? Can anything be done? And who, if anyone, can actually help?

CyberClaims exists to answer those questions in a practical way.
Rather than offering unrealistic promises, CyberClaims provides professional crypto tracing and dispute-resolution services designed to help scam victims understand what happened to their funds and what recovery options may still exist.

Below is an overview of the services CyberClaims offers and how they are used in real recovery cases.

Helping Victims Trace What Happened

One of the first challenges after a crypto scam is uncertainty. Transactions disappear into wallets, platforms stop responding, and victims are left guessing.

CyberClaims helps remove that uncertainty through crypto tracing. Using blockchain analysis tools, the team follows the movement of stolen digital assets across the blockchain. This process reveals how funds were transferred, whether they passed through multiple wallets, and whether they eventually reached a regulated exchange or service provider.

While blockchain data is public, interpreting it correctly requires experience. CyberClaims translates raw transaction data into clear findings that victims and institutions can understand.

Dispute Resolution Instead of Legal Action

CyberClaims focuses on dispute resolution, not court proceedings.
Many crypto scams involve offshore entities, fake companies, or anonymous actors, making legal action slow and impractical.

Instead, CyberClaims prepares structured evidence and submits formal dispute requests to the platforms involved in the transaction flow. This can include crypto exchanges, payment processors, banks, or fiat on-ramp services.

These disputes are designed to trigger compliance reviews, internal investigations, and in some cases, account freezes or recovery actions. This approach often produces faster results than traditional legal routes, especially in cross-border cases.

Turning Evidence Into Professional Reports

Victims often have screenshots, transaction IDs, chat logs, and emails, but on their own, these are rarely enough to prompt action.

CyberClaims converts this information into professional evidence reports. These reports explain the scam clearly, outline the transaction flow, and highlight potential compliance concerns. They are structured in a way that financial institutions and fraud teams are accustomed to reviewing.

This step is critical. Well-documented cases are far more likely to be taken seriously than informal complaints.

KYC Release Request Support

In certain situations, CyberClaims assists with KYC release requests.
These requests ask regulated platforms to disclose identity information linked to accounts that received stolen funds, subject to legal and regulatory rules.

When applicable, this service can help identify who controlled a fraudulent account and strengthen recovery or escalation efforts. Not every case qualifies, but when it does, KYC information can play a key role in moving a case forward.

Clear Case Assessments and Honest Guidance

Before any paid work begins, CyberClaims provides a case assessment. This helps victims understand whether their situation is traceable and what recovery options may realistically exist.

Importantly, CyberClaims is transparent when recovery is unlikely. The goal is not to sell false hope, but to provide clear guidance so victims can make informed decisions about next steps.

What CyberClaims Does Not Do

CyberClaims is clear about its limits.
It does not hack wallets, reverse blockchain transactions, guarantee recovery, or claim special access to law enforcement. It also never asks clients to send crypto or share private keys.

The service is built around legitimate investigation, documentation, and compliance-based dispute processes.

Who These Services Are Designed For

CyberClaims assists victims of many types of online and crypto-related fraud, including fake trading platforms, investment scams, wallet drainers, romance scams involving crypto, and recovery scams.

Each case is different, and outcomes depend on timing, transaction paths, and platform involvement. The service is designed to bring clarity and structure to what is often a confusing and stressful situation.

Summary

CyberClaims offers professional crypto tracing, evidence preparation, dispute resolution, and compliance-focused recovery support for scam victims.
Rather than making promises, the company focuses on understanding what happened, documenting the facts, and engaging the right institutions through proper channels.

For anyone who has lost funds to a crypto or online investment scam, CyberClaims provides a transparent and structured starting point through CyberClaims.net.

posts.title: How Does Crypto Tracing Work?
posts.body_html:
Many individuals mistakenly think that when money is taken (lost) in a crypto theft situation, it is lost forever and will never be able to be located. This could not be any more incorrect than if it were to say it has literally gone up in smoke (disappeared in a puff of "magic"). The truth is that every transaction that occurs with a cryptocurrency leaves behind a record (a digital footprint) that remains saved forever within the bounds of the Blockchain network, and it is only through what is called crypto tracing that Law Enforcement Agencies and Recovery/Forensic Teams can follow the route each stolen crypto asset takes through the Crypto Community until it is either recovered or "frozen" in an account for database purposes.

Throughout the upcoming sections of this paper, we will discuss how a Law Enforcement Agency utilizes this technique to recover stolen crypto funds for victims of scams and other types of Financial Crimes. We will also go into more detail about the importance of crypto tracing to victims of scams and other financial offenses in their quest to recover their funds.

Why Crypto Is Traceable

It is important to note that while cryptocurrency wallets do not show names, they are not kept hidden from view. When you make a transaction with the cryptocurrency wallet, the transaction information including the wallets sending and receiving the funds, the time stamp, the amount sent/received, and the transaction ID will all be available to the public on the blockchain. After they are written to the blockchain, this information can never be changed or deleted.

Investigators can take advantage of this information to trace where your funds went after being scammed, rather than relying on conjecture. The investigators have access to reliable records that anyone could verify themselves.

The First Step: Identifying the Starting Point

In order to track cryptocurrency transactions, it is necessary to start with something you already know. In the majority of cases, this consists of the wallet address to which you sent your funds, as well as the transaction identification number provided by the blockchain.

Using this information as a starting point, analysts are able to track where the funds have gone by examining whether or not the funds were sent to a different wallet, if they were split into multiple transactions, or if they were routed through smart contracts, mixers, and decentralised.

No matter how complex a scam is, there will be patterns that emerge over time. It is essential that we follow these patterns accurately and do not attempt to guess their nature.

Following the Transaction Trail

Once the initial transaction is identified, tracing continues step by step.
Investigators monitor how the funds move across wallets and whether they interact with known services such as crypto exchanges, payment gateways, or DeFi platforms.

This stage helps answer important questions:

  • Did the funds reach a regulated exchange
  • Were they converted into another cryptocurrency
  • Were they split across multiple wallets
  • Are they still moving or have they stopped

These details matter because recovery options depend heavily on where the funds ended up.

Why Exchanges Are Important

Crypto tracing becomes especially valuable when stolen funds reach a centralized exchange.
Most reputable exchanges operate under compliance rules and require identity verification for withdrawals. If traced funds enter one of these platforms, there may be an opportunity to request account reviews, freezes, or further investigation.

This is why timing matters. The sooner tracing begins, the greater the chance that funds are still within a system that can respond to formal requests.

Turning Data Into Evidence

Raw blockchain data is not enough on its own. Financial institutions and compliance teams need clear explanations, timelines, and supporting documentation.

Professional tracing converts transaction data into structured reports that show exactly how funds moved and why the activity is suspicious. These reports often include transaction paths, timestamps, wallet relationships, and platform involvement.

This step bridges the gap between technical blockchain data and real-world recovery action.

What Crypto Tracing Cannot Do

Crypto tracing does not reverse transactions or magically pull funds back.
It does not break encryption or access private wallets. It also does not guarantee recovery.

What it does provide is clarity. It shows what happened, where funds went, and whether legitimate recovery pathways exist. That information is essential for dispute resolution, regulatory reporting, or escalation to exchanges.

Why Professional Tracing Matters

While blockchain explorers are public, interpreting transaction flows correctly requires experience. Many scams involve multiple wallets, automated transfers, and deceptive techniques designed to confuse victims.

Professional tracing reduces errors, avoids false assumptions, and ensures that any recovery request is supported by accurate data. This is why victims often work with specialist services such as CyberClaims, which combine tracing with formal dispute processes.

Summary

Crypto tracing works by following permanent blockchain records to track stolen funds across wallets and platforms.
It identifies where assets moved, whether they reached regulated services, and what recovery options may still exist.

While tracing alone does not guarantee recovery, it is the foundation for any serious attempt to recover stolen crypto. Without it, victims are left guessing. With it, they gain clarity, evidence, and a structured path forward.

posts.title: What Should I Do If My Crypto Was Stolen?
posts.body_html:
Realising your crypto has been stolen is stressful and confusing. Many victims freeze or panic, unsure whether anything can be done. While crypto transactions cannot simply be reversed, there are practical steps you should take immediately to protect yourself and improve the chances of recovery.

This article explains what to do after crypto theft and why timing and documentation matter.

Act Immediately and Stop Further Losses

The first priority is to prevent additional damage. If you suspect your wallet or account has been compromised, stop all activity right away.

Disconnect your wallet from any websites or applications it is linked to. If you used a browser wallet, revoke permissions and move any remaining funds to a new, secure wallet. If an exchange account was involved, change your passwords and enable additional security measures.

Even small delays can give scammers more time to move funds beyond traceable points.

Preserve Evidence Before It Disappears

Documentation is critical. Many victims lose recovery opportunities simply because key information was not saved early.

You should collect and store:

  • Transaction IDs and wallet addresses
  • Screenshots of balances, transactions, and error messages
  • URLs of any websites or platforms involved
  • Emails, messages, or chat logs related to the scam

Do not rely on memory or assume platforms will keep records accessible. Evidence can disappear quickly once scam sites go offline.

Understand What Happened

Before taking further action, it is important to understand how the theft occurred. Crypto theft can happen in different ways, including fake investment platforms, phishing links, wallet drainers, or social engineering scams.

Understanding the method helps determine the correct recovery path. For example, funds stolen through a wallet drainer may require a different approach than funds sent directly to a fraudulent exchange.

Professional analysis can help identify where mistakes were exploited and what steps are still possible.

Report the Incident to the Relevant Platform

If the theft involved a crypto exchange, wallet provider, or payment service, report it as soon as possible. Many platforms have fraud or security reporting channels.

While reporting does not guarantee recovery, it creates a record and may trigger internal reviews or account monitoring. In some cases, exchanges can freeze funds if they are notified early enough.

Always use official support channels and avoid links provided by unknown third parties.

Avoid “Recovery Agents” Who Contact You

After a scam, many victims are contacted by people claiming they can recover the stolen crypto quickly. These are often secondary scams targeting people who have already lost money.

Be cautious of anyone who:

  • Contacts you first
  • Asks for upfront crypto payments
  • Claims guaranteed recovery
  • Says they work with law enforcement
  • Requests access to your wallet

Legitimate recovery services do not operate this way.

Get Professional Tracing and Recovery Guidance

Recovering stolen crypto usually requires understanding where the funds went and whether they passed through regulated platforms. This is where professional crypto tracing and dispute resolution become important.

Specialist services such as CyberClaims help victims trace transactions on the blockchain, prepare evidence, and submit structured recovery or compliance requests to exchanges and payment processors.

The sooner this process begins, the better the chances that funds are still within traceable systems.

Report to Authorities and Regulators

Even if recovery is uncertain, reporting the incident to your local cybercrime unit or financial regulator is still important. These reports help authorities identify patterns, shut down scam networks, and warn others.

In some cases, regulatory involvement can also support recovery efforts when combined with proper tracing documentation.

What Crypto Theft Does and Does Not Mean

Crypto theft does not always mean your funds are gone forever, but it also does not guarantee recovery. Outcomes depend on factors such as timing, transaction paths, and whether funds reached regulated platforms.

What matters most is acting quickly, staying organised, and using legitimate recovery channels instead of reacting emotionally.

Summary

If your crypto was stolen, act immediately to secure your accounts, preserve evidence, and understand how the theft occurred. Report the incident to any involved platforms and avoid anyone offering instant recovery.

Professional tracing and dispute resolution can help determine whether recovery options still exist. While crypto theft is serious, taking structured steps gives you the best possible chance of protecting yourself and moving forward.

For victims unsure where to start, CyberClaims.net provides a clear and professional first step toward understanding recovery options.

posts.title: Who Can Help Me Recover Funds From a Crypto Scam?
posts.body_html:
After losing money to a crypto scam, many people are left searching for help. Unfortunately, this is also the moment when victims are most vulnerable to being scammed again. The crypto recovery space is crowded with false promises, fake “experts,” and unregulated operators who claim they can recover funds instantly.

So who can actually help, and who should you avoid?

This article explains the legitimate options available to scam victims and how to identify real recovery support.

Why Crypto Scam Recovery Is Difficult

Crypto scams are often cross-border, anonymous, and technically complex. Funds may move through multiple wallets, be converted into different assets, or pass through platforms in different jurisdictions.

Because of this, traditional solutions such as calling the police or contacting a lawyer may not lead to immediate results. Recovery usually depends on tracing where funds went and whether they interacted with regulated platforms that have compliance obligations.

This is why specialised recovery support exists.

Legitimate Crypto Recovery and Dispute Services

Professional dispute-resolution and tracing services are often the most practical option for crypto scam victims. These services focus on evidence, blockchain analysis, and structured communication with financial institutions.

A legitimate service will typically:

  • Analyse blockchain transactions to trace stolen funds
  • Identify exchanges or payment providers involved
  • Prepare formal evidence reports
  • Submit recovery or compliance requests to relevant platforms
  • Communicate professionally with fraud and compliance teams

Specialist services such as CyberClaims operate within this framework, helping victims understand what happened and what recovery options may still exist.

When Exchanges and Platforms Can Help

If stolen funds reached a regulated crypto exchange, there may be an opportunity for intervention. Most reputable exchanges have fraud response and compliance departments that review documented scam reports.

Exchanges may be able to:

  • Freeze accounts linked to fraudulent activity
  • Investigate suspicious transactions
  • Provide information to regulators
  • Cooperate with recovery or dispute requests

Timing is critical. The earlier a case is reported and documented, the greater the chance that funds are still accessible within the platform.

The Role of Authorities and Regulators

Reporting a crypto scam to authorities is still important, even if recovery is uncertain. Law enforcement and financial regulators use reports to track patterns, shut down scam networks, and issue public warnings.

While authorities may not act immediately on individual cases, their involvement can support recovery efforts when combined with professional tracing and documentation.

Who You Should Avoid

Many victims are contacted by people claiming they can recover funds quickly. These are often secondary scams targeting individuals who have already lost money.

You should be extremely cautious of anyone who:

  • Contacts you first
  • Requests upfront crypto payments
  • Promises guaranteed recovery
  • Claims direct links to police or government agencies
  • Asks for wallet access or private keys

Legitimate recovery services do not operate in this way.

Why Professional Guidance Matters

Recovering funds from a crypto scam is rarely about a single action. It requires understanding transaction paths, compliance obligations, and how to communicate effectively with financial institutions.

Professional services reduce guesswork, prevent further mistakes, and provide realistic guidance based on evidence rather than emotion. Even when full recovery is not possible, victims gain clarity and documentation that helps them move forward.

Summary

Recovering funds from a crypto scam is challenging, but legitimate help does exist. The most effective support comes from professional tracing and dispute-resolution services, regulated exchanges, and official authorities working together.

Victims should avoid unsolicited recovery offers and focus on services that operate transparently, use evidence-based methods, and set realistic expectations.

For those unsure where to begin, CyberClaims.net provides a structured and professional starting point to assess recovery options after a crypto scam.

posts.title: Crypto Scam URLs to Watch Out for in 2026
posts.body_html:
Every month, CyberClaims reviews dozens of cases involving fraudulent crypto websites. By the time most victims reach out, the damage has already been done. Funds have been sent, platforms have stopped responding, and the website involved has often already disappeared.

As we move into 2026, scam URLs are becoming more convincing, more short-lived, and harder to identify at a glance. Many are designed to look like legitimate trading platforms, wallet services, or recovery companies. Others impersonate well-known crypto brands or lure users through fake airdrops and promotions.

This article outlines the types of scam URLs CyberClaims is seeing most frequently, along with real examples reported by victims, and explains how to spot dangerous domains before interacting with them.

⚠️ The URLs shown below are shared for awareness only. Do not visit or interact with them.

Fake Crypto Trading and Investment Platforms

The most common cases CyberClaims handles still involve fake investment platforms. These websites are often professionally designed, feature live price charts, and display fabricated account balances to make the platform appear legitimate.

Victims are encouraged to deposit crypto, see “profits,” and are then blocked from withdrawing unless they pay additional fees such as taxes, liquidity charges, or verification costs.

Common characteristics CyberClaims sees

  • Recently registered domains
  • Generic platform names with financial terms
  • No verifiable company registration
  • Withdrawals permanently “under review”

Example scam-style URLs reported in cases

  • novatrade-capital.com
  • globalcryptoinvest.ai
  • bitwealth-pro.net

In most cases, these sites are taken offline once enough funds are collected, only to reappear under a new domain with the same structure.

Impersonation of Legitimate Crypto Brands

Another growing category involves websites impersonating well-known crypto exchanges and wallet providers. These URLs are often shared through phishing emails, SMS messages, or social media ads claiming there is an issue with your account.

The sites closely copy official branding and are designed to steal login credentials or prompt wallet connections.

Typical impersonation patterns

  • Added words like verify, secure, support, or login
  • Slight misspellings of real brand names
  • Unfamiliar domain extensions

Examples seen in reported scams

  • binance-verify.net
  • coinbase-support.app
  • kraken-secure-login.com

Once credentials are entered, accounts are often drained within minutes.

Wallet Drainers and Fake Airdrop Pages

Wallet-draining scams have increased significantly going into 2026. These sites promote free token claims, NFT mints, or exclusive rewards and require users to connect their wallet.

Approving a transaction on these sites often grants permission for a malicious smart contract to transfer funds out automatically.

Red flags CyberClaims frequently sees

  • Countdown timers and urgency
  • “Claim now” messaging
  • No transparent project details

Example scam-style domains

  • eth-airdrop2026.xyz
  • claim-layerzero.app
  • web3-reward.site

Legitimate projects do not pressure users to approve transactions without full transparency.

Fake Recovery and Refund Websites

A particularly harmful trend CyberClaims continues to encounter is secondary recovery scams. These target people who have already lost money and are actively searching for help.

These websites claim to recover funds using “blockchain investigators,” “legal teams,” or “government partnerships.” In reality, they exist only to extract additional fees.

Common warning signs

  • Guaranteed recovery promises
  • Requests for upfront payments
  • Use of fake law enforcement imagery

Reported scam-style URLs

  • cryptoasset-recovery.org
  • blockchainrefundteam.com
  • fundsretrieval-experts.net

Victims often lose more money to these sites than in the original scam.

Why Scam URLs Change So Quickly

Scam websites are rarely meant to last. Once a domain is reported or gains negative attention, scammers abandon it and launch a new one using the same templates, scripts, and support messages.

This constant rotation is why CyberClaims focuses on patterns, transaction tracing, and evidence, not just individual URLs.

What to Do If You Interacted With a Scam URL

If you believe you used a fraudulent website:

  • Stop all communication immediately
  • Do not send additional funds
  • Save the URL, screenshots, and transaction details
  • Disconnect wallets from suspicious sites
  • Seek professional guidance as soon as possible

In some cases, tracing transactions can help determine whether funds reached regulated platforms and whether dispute options may still exist.

Summary

Crypto scam URLs in 2026 are more sophisticated, short-lived, and convincing than ever. Fake trading platforms, impersonation sites, wallet drainers, and recovery scams continue to cause significant financial losses.

Understanding common scam patterns and verifying websites before interacting with them is essential. If you have already interacted with a suspicious crypto website,CyberClaims can help analyse what happened and advise on the next steps based on evidence and transaction tracing.

How to Check a Suspicious Crypto URL

Because scam websites appear and disappear quickly, the safest way to verify a suspicious crypto URL is to check it against trusted public databases that track reported scams. We recommend reviewing any unknown or questionable website using resources such as the California Department of Financial Protection and Innovation (DFPI) Crypto Scam Tracker, Crypto Legal UK’s reported scam companies list, and CryptoScamDB, a community-maintained database of verified phishing and scam domains. These platforms are regularly updated and allow you to search URLs before interacting with them. Taking a few minutes to check a link can prevent irreversible losses.

You can find them here:

  • DFPI Crypto Scam Tracker: https://dfpi.ca.gov/consumers/crypto/crypto-scam-tracker/
  • Crypto Legal UK Scam List: https://www.cryptolegal.uk/list-of-reported-scam-companies/
  • CryptoScamDB: https://cryptoscamdb.org/

posts.title: FX Revenues Review: CONFIRMED SCAM Warning
posts.body_html:
FX Revenues Review: CONFIRMED SCAM Warning

The Bottom Line

Verdict: CONFIRMED SCAM Website: N/A Regulation: FCA

Is FX Revenues a Scam?

If you are reading this, you are likely experiencing difficulty withdrawing your funds from FX Revenues. You may feel confused, frustrated, and even betrayed. At CyberClaims, we understand the emotional toll this takes. We’re here to tell you that your suspicions are valid: FX Revenues is a confirmed scam, and we're here to help you understand why and what you can do next.

Red Flags Identified

FX Revenues exhibits numerous red flags that unequivocally point towards fraudulent activity:
  • False Promises: They lure in unsuspecting investors with promises of high returns and guaranteed profits in the Forex and CFD markets, claims that are simply unrealistic and designed to deceive.
  • Manipulative Tactics: Victims are often manipulated into signing misleading agreements they don't fully understand, effectively trapping them in unfavorable terms.
  • Unauthorized Transactions: Investors have reported unauthorized trades being executed in their accounts without their consent, leading to significant losses.
  • Withdrawal Blockages: This is the most common complaint. When investors try to withdraw their funds, they are met with endless delays, excuses, and outright refusal.
  • Account Frozen: Without valid justification, FX Revenues freezes accounts, preventing access to deposited funds.

Victims Report

The experiences shared by victims of FX Revenues paint a disturbing picture:
  • Investors report being asked to pay exorbitant fees or taxes (often around 20% of their account balance) before they can withdraw their funds. These fees are often sprung on them unexpectedly.
  • Victims describe being pressured to deposit more and more funds into their accounts, often with aggressive and coercive tactics.
  • Many have reported unauthorized trades being made on their accounts, draining their capital without their consent.
  • The most common complaint is the outright refusal or obstruction of withdrawals, leaving investors unable to access their hard-earned money.
  • Some victims have even reported unprofessional and abusive behavior from company representatives.
  • UK customers are particularly targeted, falling prey to their deceptive marketing strategies.

How the Scam Works

FX Revenues operates using a classic Forex/CFD scam modus operandi:
  1. Enticement: They lure victims with enticing advertisements and promises of quick riches in the Forex and CFD markets.
  2. Initial Investment: Victims are encouraged to make an initial investment, often a relatively small amount to build trust.
  3. Fake Profits: Initially, victims may see artificial profits on their trading platform, further incentivizing them to invest more.
  4. Pressure and Manipulation: Account managers then pressure victims to deposit larger sums, promising even greater returns.
  5. The Trap: Once a substantial amount of money has been deposited, withdrawal requests are met with resistance.
  6. Extortion: The scammer will demand fees, taxes, or other fabricated charges to supposedly release the funds.
  7. Disappearance: Eventually, the scammers disappear, leaving victims with significant financial losses.

CyberClaims Verdict

Based on the numerous red flags, regulatory warnings from the FCA, and the consistent reports of fraudulent activity, CyberClaims unequivocally concludes that FX Revenues is a confirmed scam. Their practices are designed to defraud unsuspecting investors and should be avoided at all costs.

What To Do If You Lost Money

If you have deposited funds with FX Revenues, stop making payments immediately. Do not send them any more money, regardless of their promises or threats. posts.title: Cryptocurrency Scam Recovery: Step-by-Step Guide for Victims
posts.body_html:
The promise of cryptocurrency wealth has attracted millions of investors worldwide—and unfortunately, just as many scammers looking to exploit them. If you've lost Bitcoin, Ethereum, or other cryptocurrencies to a fraudulent scheme, you're facing a uniquely challenging recovery situation.

Unlike traditional banking fraud, cryptocurrency transactions are designed to be irreversible and pseudonymous. However, "difficult" doesn't mean "impossible." This comprehensive guide explains exactly how cryptocurrency scam recovery works and the steps you can take to maximize your chances of getting your funds back.

Understanding Why Crypto Recovery Is Different

Cryptocurrency operates on blockchain technology, which creates both challenges and opportunities for scam victims:

The Challenges

  • Irreversible transactions: Once confirmed on the blockchain, crypto transfers cannot be reversed like credit card charges
  • Pseudonymous addresses: Wallet addresses don't directly reveal owner identities
  • Global and borderless: Scammers can operate from anywhere, complicating jurisdiction
  • Decentralized nature: No central authority can freeze or reverse transactions
  • Rapid fund movement: Scammers quickly move stolen crypto through multiple wallets

The Opportunities

  • Permanent public record: Every transaction is recorded forever on the blockchain
  • Traceable fund flows: Sophisticated analysis can follow money through countless transfers
  • Exchange cooperation: Legitimate exchanges increasingly cooperate with law enforcement
  • Growing regulation: More jurisdictions are implementing crypto-specific fraud laws
  • Specialized recovery tools: Blockchain forensics has become highly sophisticated

Immediate Actions After a Crypto Scam

Step 1: Stop All Transactions Immediately

Cease any further cryptocurrency transfers to the scammer. Common tactics to extract more funds include:

  • Claiming you need to pay "withdrawal fees" or "taxes"
  • Saying your account is "locked" and requires a deposit to unlock
  • Offering to "recover" your funds for an additional payment
  • Threatening legal action unless you pay more

These are all lies. Never send additional cryptocurrency to recover lost funds.

Step 2: Secure Your Remaining Assets

If scammers have any access to your wallets or accounts:

  • Transfer remaining crypto to a new, secure wallet with fresh seed phrases
  • Change passwords on all cryptocurrency exchanges
  • Enable two-factor authentication (preferably hardware-based, not SMS)
  • Revoke any smart contract approvals you may have granted
  • Check for and remove any unauthorized API connections

Step 3: Document Everything

Comprehensive documentation is essential for any recovery effort:

Transaction Information:

  • Transaction IDs (TXIDs) for all transfers to the scammer
  • Wallet addresses you sent funds to
  • Amounts and dates of each transaction
  • Screenshots of your wallet showing the transactions

Communication Records:

  • All messages with the scammer (email, chat, social media)
  • Screenshots of the fraudulent platform or website
  • Marketing materials and promises made
  • Phone numbers and names used by scammers

Platform Information:

  • The scam website URL (archive it using archive.org)
  • Screenshots of the fake platform interface
  • Any "account statements" or documents provided
  • App store listings if a mobile app was involved

Blockchain Tracing and Analysis

How Blockchain Forensics Works

Despite crypto's reputation for anonymity, blockchain analysis has become remarkably sophisticated. Forensic specialists can:

  • Track funds through hundreds of wallet transfers
  • Identify patterns linking multiple addresses to the same entity
  • Detect when funds reach regulated exchanges (where identity verification exists)
  • Connect wallet clusters to known criminal organizations
  • Provide court-admissible evidence for legal proceedings

Using Blockchain Explorers

You can begin basic tracing yourself using free blockchain explorers:

  • Bitcoin: blockchain.com, blockchair.com
  • Ethereum: etherscan.io
  • Multiple chains: blockchair.com, chainalysis.com (limited free features)

Enter the wallet address you sent funds to and follow the transaction trail. Note any exchanges the funds pass through—these are potential recovery points.

Professional Blockchain Analysis

For significant losses, professional blockchain forensics services offer:

  • Advanced clustering algorithms to identify scammer wallets
  • Real-time monitoring if funds haven't moved yet
  • Detailed reports suitable for law enforcement and legal proceedings
  • Identification of exchange accounts where funds may be frozen
  • Expert witness testimony for court cases

Reporting to Authorities

Law Enforcement Agencies

United States:

  • FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center (IC3): ic3.gov
  • Secret Service (for large-scale fraud)
  • Your local FBI field office for significant losses

United Kingdom:

  • Action Fraud: actionfraud.police.uk
  • National Crime Agency for major cases

European Union:

  • Europol's European Cybercrime Centre (EC3)
  • Your national cybercrime unit

Australia:

  • Australian Cyber Security Centre: cyber.gov.au
  • ACCC Scamwatch

International:

  • Interpol (through your local law enforcement)

Regulatory Bodies

File complaints with financial regulators, even though crypto regulation varies:

  • SEC (if the scam involved securities or investment contracts)
  • CFTC (for crypto derivatives or commodity fraud)
  • FTC (for general consumer fraud)
  • State attorneys general
  • Financial regulators in the scammer's claimed jurisdiction

Cryptocurrency Exchanges

If you can trace funds to a legitimate exchange:

  • Contact the exchange's compliance or fraud department
  • Provide transaction IDs and wallet addresses
  • Include your police report number
  • Request they freeze associated accounts

Major exchanges like Coinbase, Binance, and Kraken have compliance teams that cooperate with law enforcement and may freeze accounts linked to fraud.

Legal Recovery Options

Civil Litigation

Even when scammers seem anonymous, legal action may be possible:

  • John Doe lawsuits: File against unknown defendants, then use discovery to identify them
  • Asset freezing orders: Courts can order exchanges to freeze accounts
  • Norwich Pharmacal orders: Compel third parties to reveal scammer information
  • International enforcement: Judgments can sometimes be enforced across borders

Class Action Lawsuits

For large-scale scams affecting many victims, class actions offer:

  • Shared legal costs among victims
  • Greater resources for investigation
  • More attention from law enforcement
  • Stronger negotiating position

Search online for existing class actions related to your scam—joining an existing case is often more effective than starting alone.

Criminal Restitution

If law enforcement prosecutes the scammers, victims may receive restitution:

  • Register as a victim with the prosecuting agency
  • Provide documentation of your losses
  • Attend relevant court proceedings
  • File a victim impact statement

Recovery Through Exchanges and Payment Processors

If You Purchased Crypto Before Sending to Scammers

The exchange where you bought cryptocurrency may be able to help:

  • Report the fraud to their support team
  • They may have additional information about where funds went
  • Some exchanges offer victim assistance programs
  • They can flag associated addresses in their systems

Credit Card Chargebacks for Crypto Purchases

If you used a credit card to buy cryptocurrency that you then sent to scammers:

  • The crypto purchase itself may be eligible for chargeback
  • Contact your card issuer immediately
  • Explain the full fraud scenario
  • Provide all documentation

Success rates vary, but it's worth attempting, especially for recent transactions.

Avoiding Recovery Scams

Victims of crypto scams are prime targets for "recovery scams"—fraudsters who promise to retrieve your lost funds for an upfront fee, then disappear with that money too.

Red Flags of Recovery Scams

  • Unsolicited contact claiming they can recover your funds
  • Guarantees of recovery (no one can guarantee this)
  • Requests for upfront payment before any work
  • Claims of "special access" to blockchain or exchanges
  • Pressure to act quickly
  • Requests for remote access to your computer
  • Asking for your wallet seed phrases

Legitimate Recovery Services

Reputable recovery assistance typically:

  • Never guarantees specific outcomes
  • Provides clear explanation of their process
  • Has verifiable track record and reviews
  • Offers transparent fee structures
  • Never asks for seed phrases or wallet access
  • Works with law enforcement, not around them

Realistic Expectations for Recovery

Factors Affecting Recovery Success

  • Speed of action: Faster reporting improves chances
  • Amount lost: Larger losses may justify more resources
  • Where funds went: Funds reaching regulated exchanges are more recoverable
  • Scammer sophistication: Professional criminals are harder to catch
  • Jurisdiction: Some countries cooperate more than others
  • Documentation quality: Better evidence supports stronger cases

Timeline Expectations

  • Exchange freezes: Days to weeks if funds are identified quickly
  • Law enforcement investigation: Months to years
  • Civil litigation: 1-3 years typically
  • Criminal prosecution: 2-5 years in complex cases

How CyberClaims.net Can Help

Recovering cryptocurrency from scammers requires specialized knowledge of blockchain technology, legal procedures, and the complex landscape of crypto regulation. CyberClaims.net has helped numerous victims of cryptocurrency fraud navigate this challenging process.

Our services include:

  • Initial case assessment and recovery strategy development
  • Guidance on reporting to appropriate authorities
  • Coordination with blockchain analysis specialists
  • Support for chargeback and banking recovery processes
  • Connection with legal resources for civil recovery options

Every case is unique, and we provide honest assessments of recovery possibilities based on your specific circumstances. If you've lost cryptocurrency to a scam, contact CyberClaims.net today for a confidential consultation.

Conclusion

Cryptocurrency scam recovery is challenging but not impossible. The blockchain's transparency means your funds can be traced, and the growing cooperation between exchanges, law enforcement, and regulators is improving recovery rates.

Take immediate action to document everything, report to authorities, and explore all recovery avenues. Don't let embarrassment or hopelessness prevent you from fighting for your funds. Many victims have successfully recovered some or all of their losses through persistent, informed action.

The scammers are counting on you to give up. Don't give them that satisfaction.

__________________________________________________

Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal or financial advice. Cryptocurrency recovery outcomes vary significantly based on individual circumstances, and no recovery can be guaranteed.

posts.title: Click2Sell.co Review: CONFIRMED SCAM - 2026 Warning
posts.body_html:
Click2Sell.co Review: CONFIRMED SCAM - 2026 Warning

The Bottom Line

Verdict: CONFIRMED SCAM Website: www.click2sell.co Regulation: Multiple warnings from independent scam reviewers; Unregulated platform.

Is Click2Sell.co a Scam?

If you are reading this, you might be struggling to withdraw your earnings or even your initial investment from Click2Sell.co. Perhaps you were lured in by promises of easy passive income through “ad flipping.” We understand your frustration and concern. At CyberClaims.net, we have investigated Click2Sell.co and can confirm: it's a scam. Our team has seen a disturbing pattern of deceptive practices, leaving unsuspecting individuals with empty promises and lighter wallets.

Red Flags Identified

Click2Sell.co exhibits several glaring red flags that should have raised alarm bells from the start. One of the most prominent is the unrealistic promise of generating passive income with little to no marketing experience. They claim you can simply buy ad packages and watch the profits roll in. This "ad flipping" business model is highly suspect and, in our expert opinion, bogus and fraudulent.

Furthermore, they pressure individuals to purchase increasingly expensive ad packages, ranging from $250 to a staggering $25,000, all without any guarantee of returns. We've also uncovered concerning connections between Click2Sell.co and previously shut-down scam networks like BannerBit and ClicksDealer. Finally, the lack of transparent ownership or employee information further solidifies our conclusion that Click2Sell.co is operating as a deceptive entity.

Victims Report

The experiences of victims paint a grim picture. Investors report being asked to pay exorbitant “tax” or “withdrawal” fees before they can access their supposed earnings. Many have lost thousands of dollars, only to be met with empty promises and a barrage of excuses from Click2Sell.co representatives.

Even more disturbing, some victims report potential identity theft and unauthorized credit card transactions after providing their personal information to the platform. The financial and emotional damage inflicted by Click2Sell.co is significant and devastating. If this sounds familiar and you've been impacted, contact CyberClaims immediately for a consultation. Our expert team can help you understand your options.

How the Scam Works

Click2Sell.co, like many "ad flipping" scams, operates on a simple but insidious premise. They entice victims with the allure of effortless passive income. Investors are led to believe that they can purchase ad packages, and Click2Sell.co will then "flip" these ads for a profit, generating consistent returns for the investor.

In reality, there is little to no evidence of legitimate ad flipping activity. Instead, the funds from new investors are likely used to pay off earlier investors, creating a Ponzi scheme that eventually collapses. When investors attempt to withdraw their earnings, they are often met with delays, excuses, and demands for additional payments before funds can be released. This pattern continues until the scam operators disappear with the remaining funds, leaving victims with significant losses.

CyberClaims Verdict

Based on our thorough investigation, the numerous red flags, and the consistent reports from victims, CyberClaims.net definitively concludes that Click2Sell.co is a scam. Their deceptive business model, lack of transparency, and connection to previous fraudulent schemes make it clear that their primary objective is to defraud unsuspecting investors.

What To Do If You Lost Money

If you have deposited funds with Click2Sell.co, stop making payments immediately. Do not send any more money, regardless of the promises made by Click2Sell.co representatives. Preserve all communication records, including emails, chat logs, and transaction details. We strongly advise you to file a free complaint through our website. This crucial step helps us gather information and potentially build a stronger case for recovery. We understand the emotional toll this has taken, and we are here to help you navigate this difficult situation.

References

  • Click2Sell.co Review - Class A Scam: https://freeaffiliatemarketingbusiness.com
  • CyberScamReview - Click2Sell Investigation: https://cyberscamreview.com
  • CyberTrace - Ad Flipping Scams: https://cybertrace.com.au
